criteria.evidence: Constraints: Days until interview: 10; Rounds: recruiter screen (30m) -> hiring-manager screen (45m: background + product-sense) -> onsite (virtual): product-sense case / analytical/metrics case / execution case / behavioral
criteria.reason: Timeline (10 days) and full round structure captured verbatim from the JD and actually used to drive the day-by-day cadence (Day 1..Day 10/Morning-of).

criteria.evidence: Row 1 build-stories maps to 'recruiter + HM screen + behavioral onsite'; Row 3 build-curriculum maps to 'HM product-sense + all 3 onsite cases'; cadence front-loads stories+honest framing (Day 1) and research (Day 2) before deeper PM-craft.
criteria.reason: Sequencing matches the JD's actual round shape: the near-term recruiter/HM screens (stories + honest switch framing + research) are front-loaded, and onsite-case prep is staged later. Round-aware as required.

criteria.evidence: Priority queue table columns 'Action | Skill to run | Maps to | Must-do?', e.g. 'Close PM-craft gaps... | build-curriculum | HM product-sense + all 3 onsite cases | check'.
criteria.reason: Every action names a concrete skill and the specific round/gap it serves; the dominant PM-craft gap is correctly routed to build-curriculum as a must-do (matches the expectations watch-item).

criteria.evidence: 'Done looks like' includes: 'She delivers the honest career-switch framing in one breath without apologizing, then pivots to transferable evidence' and 'She can run a basic product-sense pass (user -> problem -> prioritized solution -> success metric) on a healthcare-workflow problem unprompted.'
criteria.reason: Concrete, behavior-anchored readiness bar tied to real numbers and the honest-switch narrative; not platitudes.

criteria.evidence: Row 6 'analytical/metrics case + light SQL drill'; Day 7 'SQL beyond simple SELECTs (GROUP BY, joins)'; build-curriculum for PM fundamentals is the central must-do.
criteria.reason: No mis-prioritization: SQL is kept light (the JD lists it as 'a plus'), no coding grind is prescribed, and prep weight goes to PM craft and product-sense, which is the real gap. No rubber-stamping of readiness.
